pm_decision_extract.decision_or_assessment: Quality-long candidate evaluation post-60d selloff; hard fundamental gate failure blocks entry
evidence_used: corporate_calendar.earnings (recent beats: 0.55 vs 0.49 est; 0.56 vs 0.42 est)
sentiment_community_ownership_evidence.fmp_sentiment (3 SB, 2 B, 11 H, 2 S, 3 SS as of 2025-03-01)
pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: BF-B (Brown-Forman Inc., Class B) is a mature consumer-staples / beverage company that has declined 21.3% over 60 days. Valuation metrics (PE 16.0, PB 3.17, EV/EBITDA 12.75) appear fair-to-attractive, and profitability indicators (20.6% net margin, 19.9% ROE, 0.67 debt-to-equity) are solid. Recent earnings show mixed execution (Q4 beat, Q3 miss). However, the hard fundamental gate (§3.8) FAILS on two critical inputs: (1) eps_ttm is null (cannot verify > 0), and (2) operating_margin_ttm is null (cannot verify > 0). Per RULES.md §3.8, when hard gates fail, the recommended action collapses to no_trade / watch / needs_more_evidence. The upstream quality_long agent correctly recommends no_trade due to gate failure and data insufficiency. The macro regime is Overheat / Moderately Restrictive (stress_score=2), which caps equity allocation at 20% and mandates strict quality/valuation caution — consistent with a defensive posture. Analyst sentiment is mixed (11 Hold dominant; 5 bullish vs 5 bearish). The 60-day decline appears macro/sector-driven rather than company-specific, but without confirmed positive TTM earnings and operating margin, the thesis cannot be validated. Risk agent confirms no hard-veto conditions are tripped (sleeve cap, lookahead, hindsight all clear). The decision is no_trade pending PIT-safe fundamental data confirmation.
invalidation_conditions: If fundamental_snapshot is populated with PIT-safe eps_ttm > 0 and operating_margin_ttm > 0, the hard gate would pass and thesis could be re-evaluated.
If macro regime transitions out of Overheat, equity allocation cap would relax and valuation caution would ease.
If analyst sentiment shifts materially bullish (more Buy/Strong Buy ratings), conviction would increase.
If news catalyst emerges (positive earnings guidance, strategic announcement), narrative_price_gap would shift.

60-day decline (-21.3%) may reflect macro/sector headwinds, but without confirmed fundamentals, recovery thesis is unvalidated.
Ownership data (13F) unavailable; cannot assess smart-money support or crowding risk.
suggested_improvements: Populate fundamental_snapshot with PIT-safe TTM earnings and operating margin from most recent 10-Q or 10-K filing.
Retrieve 13F ownership data to assess institutional support and insider positioning.
Obtain news/catalyst clarity for the 60-day decline (macro/sector vs. company-specific).
Monitor macro regime for transition out of Overheat; if regime shifts, re-evaluate equity allocation cap.
Track analyst sentiment for upgrade/downgrade signals.
Wire alternative-data adapters (consumer_traffic_adapter for beverage footfall, valuation_context for DCF validation).

The valuation_snapshot uses TTM ratios (not PIT-safe), which are descriptive but not suitable for hard-gate inputs. The narrative_price_gap_assessment is not_evaluated (pending agent LLM), limiting context on the 60-day selloff origin. These gaps are architectural (v1 generator limitations) rather than data-fetch failures.

Re-run quality-long assessment once hard gates can be verified.

Valuation is fair (PE 16.0, PB 3.17) but macro regime (Overheat, equity cap 20%) mandates strict quality discipline. Analyst sentiment mixed (11 Hold, 5 bullish, 5 bearish). 60-day decline (-21.3%) likely macro/sector-driven, but thesis cannot be validated without PIT-safe earnings/margin confirmation. Risk agent confirms no hard-veto conditions tripped. Decision: no_trade pending fundamental data population.

narrative_event.reasoning_summary: BF-B exhibits a 3.28% 5-day return and 1.13x relative volume vs 20d average as of 2025-02-28 EOD. The evidence packet shows SEC 8-K filing confirmation (filing_date 2025-01-14, Item 2.05 — costs associated with exit or disposal activities) but the news_event_summary block is data_unavailable with 0 items in the decision window (20 items were dropped post-cutoff). The filing_confirmation.full_text.sec_8k_fulltext is in stub mode and does not contain ground-truth filing text. Wikipedia pageviews show elevated attention (peak 276 views on 2025-02-26, elevated from baseline ~140-160 range), suggesting some public interest spike, but this is a weak alternative-data signal and does not directly corroborate a specific corporate catalyst. The corporate_calendar shows no upcoming earnings or dividend events within ±5 trading days of decision_timestamp. The fundamental_snapshot is marked insufficient_evidence, and the fundamental_gate_38 fails on eps_ttm_or_net_income_ttm_not_positive and operating_margin_not_available. Without access to the actual 8-K text (stub mode prevents ground-truth extraction), the specific nature of the 'costs associated with exit or disposal activities' cannot be verified. The price action (modest 5d gain, elevated volume) is consistent with a modest positive catalyst, but the absence of corroborating news, the stub-mode filing text, and the lack of fundamental data make it impossible to classify the catalyst with confidence. The narrative is VAGUE because the only concrete evidence is the 8-K Item 2.05 classification, which is a legal category, not a business-specific claim. No dollar amounts, customer names, or specific dates beyond the filing date are available.
invalidation_conditions: If the 8-K Item 2.05 text (when retrieved in non-stub mode) reveals the 'exit or disposal' is immaterial or routine, the catalyst specificity drops further.
If the Wikipedia pageviews spike is unrelated to BF-B corporate news (e.g., related to the generic 'BF' article), the alternative-data signal is invalidated.
If subsequent earnings or guidance revisions contradict the positive price action, the catalyst may be momentum-driven rather than value-creating.

Obtain fundamental data (income statement, balance sheet) to assess whether the 'exit or disposal' is accretive or dilutive to earnings.
Clarify whether the Wikipedia pageviews spike is ticker-specific or generic 'BF' article noise.
Monitor next earnings call for management commentary on the Item 2.05 event and its financial impact.

narrative_event.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet structure does not include a dedicated 'company_claim_text' field extracted from the 8-K body. The filing_confirmation block confirms the 8-K exists and its Item classification (2.05 — costs associated with exit or disposal), but the full_text sub-block is in stub mode. This creates a structural gap: the agent can confirm a filing exists but cannot extract the specific narrative claim from it. For a robust catalyst classification, the packet should include either (a) the full 8-K text as a parsed field, or (b) a pre-extracted 'company_claim' summary from the filing. The current design forces the agent to reason from the Item classification alone, which is a legal category, not a business narrative.
recommended_followups: Retrieve sec_8k_fulltext in live mode (not stub) to extract the actual narrative claim and any quantitative disclosures.
Cross-check the 8-K filing date (2025-01-14) against the price action timeline to confirm temporal alignment.
Obtain the most recent earnings report (2024-12-05 per corporate_calendar) and compare guidance/commentary to the Item 2.05 event.
If the 'exit or disposal' involves a material asset or business unit, request investor relations clarification on financial impact.
Monitor analyst sentiment (fmp_sentiment shows 3 Strong Buy, 2 Buy, 11 Hold, 2 Sell, 3 Strong Sell as of 2025-03-01) for any post-filing revisions.

alt_data_verify.decision_or_assessment: Alternative-data verification of narrative claims against hard-to-fake evidence
alt_data_verify.reasoning_summary: BF-B (Brown-Forman, consumer staples / beverages) is a mature, dividend-paying company with stable fundamentals. The evidence packet contains no narrative claim to verify (news_event_summary.status=data_unavailable, 0 items post-cutoff). In the absence of a specific narrative, I assess the alternative-data footprint against what a legitimate company in this sector should produce. Wikipedia pageviews show modest, stable attention (95–276 views/day in Feb–Mar 2025, mean ~155), consistent with a large-cap consumer brand with no recent surge or meme activity. SEC filings are present and current (8-K filed 2025-01-14, Item 2.05 material agreement). Analyst sentiment is stable (3 Strong Buy, 2 Buy, 11 Hold, 2 Sell, 3 Strong Sell as of 2025-03-01), showing no recent shift. Form 4 insider activity is absent in the 90-day lookback (no insider purchases or sales), which is neutral for a mature company. Valuation is moderate (PE 16.0, EV/EBITDA 12.8, PB 3.2), consistent with a quality consumer staple. Price action shows +3.3% return over 5 days and 1.13x relative volume, suggesting normal trading. Macro regime is Overheat/Moderately Restrictive (stress_score=2), which caps equity allocation but does not invalidate quality holdings. The absence of corroborating alt-data (GitHub, clinical trials, patent activity) is not informative for a beverage company—these are not sector-expected signals. The structural absence of a news catalyst or narrative claim means there is no bear thesis to verify. The company's fundamentals, filings, and market positioning are consistent with a stable, mature dividend payer. No hard-to-fake evidence contradicts this profile.
invalidation_conditions: If a material negative news event (customer loss, regulatory action, guidance cut) emerges post-cutoff and is corroborated by SEC filing or credible news source, the stable narrative would be invalidated.
If insider selling accelerates or Form 4 shows executive departures, confidence in stability would decline.
If Wikipedia pageviews spike to >500/day sustained, or analyst downgrades cluster, narrative would shift to elevated attention.

Add insider-activity trend analysis (Form 4 rolling 30/60/90-day aggregates) to detect executive confidence shifts.

alt_data_verify.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet contains no explicit narrative claim (news_event_summary is empty post-cutoff). Agent 03's role is to verify narrative against evidence, but in this case the narrative is absent. The assessment defaults to structural verification: does the alt-data footprint match what a legitimate company in this sector should produce? For BF-B (beverages), the absence of GitHub, clinical trials, and patent activity is not informative. The presence of stable Wikipedia attention, current SEC filings, and moderate valuation is consistent with a mature dividend payer. The lack of a news catalyst or analyst downgrade is itself a signal of stability, not a gap.
recommended_followups: Monitor for news catalyst (earnings surprise, guidance change, M&A, regulatory action) that would trigger a narrative shift.
Track Form 4 insider activity for signs of executive confidence change (purchases would support, sales would challenge).
Implement consumer-traffic or brand-IP alt-data to provide sector-specific corroboration of market position.
Resolve sec_13f adapter to assess ownership concentration and smart-money positioning.
Upgrade sec_8k_fulltext extraction to verify material-agreement details and assess strategic implications.
alt_data_verify.narrative_price_gap_rationale: No explicit narrative claim present in evidence packet (news_event_summary.status=data_unavailable, 0 items post-cutoff). Price action (+3.3% 5d return, 1.13x relative volume) is consistent with normal trading for a stable dividend payer. Macro regime (Overheat/Moderately Restrictive) does not suggest imminent repricing. Without a narrative claim, gap assessment is not applicable.
alt_data_verify.adapter_selection_reason: Wikipedia pageviews is the only industry-agnostic alt-data adapter with rows for BF-B (30 rows, Feb 2–Mar 3, 2025). Industry-specific adapters (consumer_traffic_adapter, retail_restaurants_consumer) are not_connected. Default fallback (wikipedia_pageviews) provides attention baseline but is not sector-specific for beverages.
alt_data_verify.hard_to_fake_signal_summary: SEC filings (8-K, Form 4) are hard-to-fake and present. 8-K filed 2025-01-14 (Item 2.05 material agreement) is current and official. Form 4 shows zero insider transactions in 90-day lookback, which is neutral (no signal of executive confidence or concern). Analyst ratings (FMP T2 source) are stable and show no recent downgrade cluster. Wikipedia pageviews are stable (95–276 views/day, mean ~155), consistent with a large-cap brand with no meme activity or surge. These signals collectively support a narrative of stability, not a catalyst for repricing.
industry_specific_evidence_used: SEC filings (8-K, Form 4) — standard for all public companies, especially relevant for material agreements and insider activity.
Analyst sentiment (FMP grades-historical) — T2 source, stable distribution (3 SB, 2 B, 11 H, 2 S, 3 SS as of 2025-03-01).
Dividend history (corporate_calendar) — quarterly dividends (~0.22 per share) consistent with mature dividend payer.
Valuation metrics (PE 16.0, EV/EBITDA 12.8, PB 3.2) — moderate for quality consumer staple.
industry_specific_evidence_missing: Consumer-traffic alt-data (store visits, foot traffic, brand IP filings) — would provide sector-specific corroboration of market position.
Supply-chain signals (supplier press releases, logistics data) — relevant for beverages but not wired.
Brand-sentiment aggregation (consumer review platforms, social listening) — would detect customer satisfaction shifts.
Regulatory filings (FDA, TTB alcohol labeling) — relevant for beverages but not in alt-data manifest.

network_effect.reasoning_summary: BF-B (Brown-Forman Inc., Class B) is a mature consumer-goods / spirits manufacturer. The three-layer network-effect framework yields: Layer 1 (hard-to-fake evidence) is EMPTY — no GitHub developer ecosystem (unmapped ticker), no SEC-disclosed operating metrics for network dynamics (DAU/MAU/take-rate/marketplace GMV), no patent activity wired. Layer 2 (attention) shows modest Wikipedia pageview activity (126–276 views/day in Feb 2025, peak 276 on 2025-02-26) and stable analyst sentiment (mostly Hold/Sell/StrongSell distribution, no material change over 3+ years). Layer 3 (financial confirmation) is STALE/UNAVAILABLE — fundamental_snapshot has insufficient_evidence status; gate 38 fails on missing eps_ttm and operating_margin. The business model is a traditional consumer-packaged-goods / spirits company with brand moats (Jack Daniel's, Woodford Reserve, etc.), NOT a platform or network-effect business. The Wikipedia pageview spike in late Feb is consistent with normal brand attention, not network compounding. Analyst sentiment is flat and mixed (3 Strong Buy, 2 Buy, 11 Hold, 2 Sell, 3 Strong Sell as of 2025-03-01). No evidence of users-attract-users, supplier/developer ecosystem growth, or pricing-power expansion from network effects. Classification: ABSENT (no genuine network effect present). The business is a mature, branded consumer-goods company with traditional moats (brand equity, distribution, scale), not network economics.
invalidation_conditions: If Layer 1 evidence (GitHub developer ecosystem or SEC-disclosed operating metrics for network dynamics) becomes available and shows strong growth, classification would shift to emerging or strong_genuine.
If Layer 3 financial data becomes PIT-safe and shows accelerating revenue/margin expansion correlated with user/supplier cohort growth, classification could upgrade to emerging.

If news_event_summary reveals a material platform/ecosystem pivot (e.g., 'BF launches digital marketplace for spirits retailers'), Layer 2 attention would be re-evaluated in context of Layer 1 evidence.
risk_flags: Layer 1 completely absent — no developer ecosystem, no SEC-disclosed network metrics, no patent activity.
Layer 3 stale/unavailable — fundamental_snapshot insufficient_evidence; gate 38 fails.
Sector mismatch — BF-B is a consumer-goods / spirits manufacturer, not a software/platform/marketplace/communication business where network effects are structurally plausible.
Wikipedia pageview spike (late Feb 2025) is modest and consistent with normal brand attention, not network compounding signal.
Analyst sentiment flat and mixed over 3+ years; no material upgrade or downgrade correlated with network narrative.
suggested_improvements: Confirm BF-B's business model classification in the system — if it is indeed a consumer-goods / spirits company, network-effect analysis is not applicable; the framework should skip this ticker or flag it as 'sector_mismatch'.

If BF-B has launched a digital platform or marketplace (e.g., direct-to-consumer spirits sales, retailer network), wire Layer 1 evidence: GitHub (if open-source components exist), SEC-disclosed transaction volume / seller cohort metrics, patent filings.
Populate fundamental_snapshot with PIT-safe income/balance/cashflow rows to enable Layer 3 financial confirmation.
Expand news_event_summary lookback window or lower cutoff threshold to capture any platform/ecosystem announcements.

If this is a surge-short candidate, the absence of Layer 1 evidence on a ticker with no public platform/network narrative is corroborating bear evidence — flag as 'narrative_hype' if Layer 2 attention spikes without Layer 1 support.

network_effect.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The three-layer framework (§10.15) is sector-agnostic by design, but BF-B's business model (consumer-goods / spirits) is structurally incompatible with network-effect economics. The framework correctly yields 'absent' classification, but the system should ideally pre-filter tickers by sector and skip network-effect analysis for non-platform businesses. The Wikipedia pageview spike is a Layer 2 attention signal, but without Layer 1 evidence it is correctly classified as insufficient for 'narrative_hype' — it is simply normal brand attention.
recommended_followups: Confirm BF-B's business model and sector classification in the system.
If BF-B has a digital platform or marketplace component, wire Layer 1 adapters (GitHub, SEC operating metrics, patents).
Populate fundamental_snapshot with PIT-safe financial data to enable Layer 3 confirmation.
For surge-short candidates: if Layer 2 attention spikes without Layer 1 support, flag as 'narrative_hype' and do not justify holding short past agentic exit point (§10.14 R-COVER-02).

valuation.reasoning_summary: BF-B (Berkshire Hathaway Inc. Class B) presents a FAIR valuation profile at the decision timestamp. The P/E of 16.01x is moderate for a quality holding company with strong profitability metrics (20.58% net margin, 59.46% gross margin, 19.93% ROE). The P/B of 3.17x and P/S of 3.28x are reasonable given the company's earnings power and balance-sheet strength (current ratio 2.77, debt-to-equity 0.67). EV/EBITDA of 12.75x is in the mid-range for a mature, high-quality business. The stock has declined 21.3% over 60 days, providing some margin of safety from recent highs. However, the macro regime is OVERHEAT with strict quality and valuation caution discipline (equity cap 20%, stress_score=2). The fundamental_gate_38 failure (eps_ttm_or_net_income_ttm_not_positive, operating_margin_not_available) signals that underlying fundamental inputs are not available in the snapshot, which is a data gap rather than a negative earnings signal — the corporate calendar shows recent earnings beats (Dec 2024: 0.55 EPS vs 0.49 est; Jun 2024: 0.56 vs 0.42 est), confirming the company is profitable. The negative PEG ratio (-0.88) is anomalous and likely reflects data quality issues (negative growth input or calculation error). Analyst sentiment is mixed-to-neutral (3 SB + 2 B = 5 bullish vs 2 S + 3 SS = 5 bearish, with 11 Hold). In an Overheat regime, even fair multiples warrant caution; the 60-day drawdown provides modest margin of safety. The valuation is neither cheap nor expensive — it reflects a quality business trading at reasonable multiples in a restrictive macro environment.
invalidation_conditions: If fundamental_snapshot is populated and reveals negative earnings or deteriorating margins, the fair assessment would shift to expensive or very_expensive.
If macro regime transitions from Overheat to Crisis, the margin-of-safety bonus would increase, potentially shifting to attractive.
If analyst sentiment deteriorates materially (more Sell/Strong Sell ratings), fair could shift to expensive.
If the 60-day drawdown reverses sharply (>+10% rally) without fundamental improvement, valuation could appear expensive on momentum.

Retrieve insider Form 4 activity and 13F positioning to assess smart-money support or selling pressure.
Clarify the macro regime's equity allocation cap (20%) and confirm whether BF-B qualifies as a strict-quality exception (e.g., Berkshire's fortress balance sheet).

quality_long.decision_or_assessment: Quality-long thesis evaluation for BF-B following 60-day selloff (-21.3%)
evidence_used: sentiment_community_ownership_evidence.fmp_sentiment (analyst ratings: 3 SB, 2 B, 11 H, 2 S, 3 SS as of 2025-03-01)

quality_long.reasoning_summary: BF-B has experienced a 21.3% decline over 60 days, triggering quality-long thesis review. Valuation metrics (PE 16.0, PB 3.17, EV/EBITDA 12.75) appear moderately attractive relative to historical norms for a consumer staples / beverage company. Profitability is evident: 59.5% gross margin, 20.6% net margin, 19.9% ROE, and 9.7% ROA indicate a healthy franchise. Debt-to-equity of 0.67 and current ratio of 2.77 signal sound financial health. Recent earnings (Q4 2024: 0.55 EPS beat 0.49 est.; Q3 2024: 0.41 EPS miss 0.46 est.) show mixed execution. Analyst sentiment is mixed-to-neutral (3 SB + 2 B vs. 2 S + 3 SS; 11 Hold dominant). However, the hard fundamental gate (§3.8 FAIL-CLOSED rule) FAILS on two counts: (1) eps_ttm is not positive (null in fundamental_snapshot), and (2) operating_margin_ttm is not available (null). Per RULES.md §3.8, when ANY hard gate fails, recommended_action MUST collapse to 'watch', 'no_trade', or 'needs_more_evidence'. The absence of TTM earnings and operating margin data is not a defensive penalty but a genuine data gap that prevents verification of the first gate (eps_ttm > 0 strict positive). Macro regime is Overheat / Moderately Restrictive (stress_score=2), which caps equity allocation at 20% and mandates strict quality/valuation caution — consistent with a hold posture. The selloff appears mixed: macro headwinds (inverted yield curve, elevated policy rate) + sector rotation (consumer discretionary pressure) + company-specific (Q3 revenue miss) all plausible. Valuation is fair-to-attractive on multiples, but without confirmed positive TTM earnings and operating margin, the thesis cannot be validated as 'intact' per the gate logic.

pm.reasoning_summary: BF-B (Brown-Forman Inc., Class B) is a mature consumer-staples / beverage company that has declined 21.3% over 60 days. Valuation metrics (PE 16.0, PB 3.17, EV/EBITDA 12.75) appear fair-to-attractive, and profitability indicators (20.6% net margin, 19.9% ROE, 0.67 debt-to-equity) are solid. Recent earnings show mixed execution (Q4 beat, Q3 miss). However, the hard fundamental gate (§3.8) FAILS on two critical inputs: (1) eps_ttm is null (cannot verify > 0), and (2) operating_margin_ttm is null (cannot verify > 0). Per RULES.md §3.8, when hard gates fail, the recommended action collapses to no_trade / watch / needs_more_evidence. The upstream quality_long agent correctly recommends no_trade due to gate failure and data insufficiency. The macro regime is Overheat / Moderately Restrictive (stress_score=2), which caps equity allocation at 20% and mandates strict quality/valuation caution — consistent with a defensive posture. Analyst sentiment is mixed (11 Hold dominant; 5 bullish vs 5 bearish). The 60-day decline appears macro/sector-driven rather than company-specific, but without confirmed positive TTM earnings and operating margin, the thesis cannot be validated. Risk agent confirms no hard-veto conditions are tripped (sleeve cap, lookahead, hindsight all clear). The decision is no_trade pending PIT-safe fundamental data confirmation.
